hero.title: Open Claw: Make AI Beyond Chat
hero.subtitle: Open Source AI Bot Framework · 10-Minute Quick Start · Fully Open Source

what.title: What is OpenClaw Bot?
what.subtitle: Open Source AI Bot Framework That Actually Works for You
what.coreDesc1: OpenClaw is an open-source AI Bot framework that makes AI do more than just chat—it actually does the work for you. It's your AI execution assistant that can complete real tasks like file management, content creation, and automated deployment on your local computer.
what.coreDesc2: Simply put: ChatGPT tells you how to do it, OpenClaw just does it for you.
what.frameworkDesc: This is an open-source AI assistant framework that accepts natural language commands and executes real tasks on your local computer or server.
what.frameworkDesc2: It's not just a simple chatbot, but an agent that can truly take action and do things for you. It quickly gained popularity in tech communities, social media, and developer circles, attracting tens of thousands of developers and reaching millions of visits in a short time.
what.jarvis: Many people describe it as a personal JARVIS from Iron Man after using it—it not only understands your commands, but actually completes tasks for you.
what.whyDesc: Before this framework, people were used to using various AI chatbots. But soon, a huge gap emerged:
what.compareQuote: AI is smart, but why am I still so busy?
what.answer: It gives a very direct answer: Because you're using the wrong form. Not Chatbot, but Agent.
what.evolutionDesc: Former names: Clawdbot → Moltbot → Now OpenClaw
what.evolutionNote: Many early tutorials and videos may use clawdbot or moltbot, searching these keywords can find more historical content.
what.evolutionWarning: moltbook is a common typo, not the project name.

features.title: AI Bot Core Features
features.subtitle: Six core capabilities to make AI Bot truly your productivity assistant
features.intro: This section introduces the framework's core capabilities and features, and how to use it in different scenarios to improve productivity.
features.goalDesc: You give a goal, it breaks down steps and executes itself. You only say what you want, it figures out how to do it
features.actionDesc: Can operate file system, execute commands, call tools. It can read/write your local files, execute shell/scripts, open web pages to scrape data, call APIs, connect third-party tools
features.memoryDesc: Remembers previous tasks and context. It remembers what you've done before and can carry context between tasks
features.controlDesc: Every step is traceable, auditable, manageable. Behavior is traceable, every step can be rolled back, tool calls are explicit, you can interrupt/redirect anytime
features.modelDesc: Supports OpenAI, Claude, Google Gemini, OpenRouter, local models (via Ollama) and more. Use GPT today, switch to Claude tomorrow, connect local models the day after
features.openDesc: Code is open source, self-hostable, customizable. Not a product, but an Agent operating system prototype

install.title: OpenClaw Install Guide · 10-Minute Quick Start
install.subtitle: Just four steps to complete installation (formerly Clawdbot/Moltbot)
install.step1Item1: A computer — macOS, Linux, or Windows (WSL2)
install.step1Item3: Package manager: npm (included), pnpm, or bun
install.step1Item4: LLM API Key: Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenRouter, or local model
install.step1Version: Node version needs to be ≥22. If version is too low, download the latest LTS from nodejs.org.
install.step2Desc2: Open your command line (terminal) and execute the following openclaw install command:
install.step2Note: Recommend using -g for global installation, so you can use openclaw command in any directory. If you encounter permission issues, macOS/Linux may need to use sudo.
install.step2Recommend: Recommended method: npm installation is the simplest and quickest openclaw install method. The next openclaw onboard wizard will automatically complete all configurations, including API Key, Gateway daemon, and workspace setup.
install.step3Desc: Run the wizard command to start configuration:
install.step3Item2: Configure API Key (supports Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenRouter, etc.)
install.step3Item3: Install Gateway daemon service (runs in background)
install.step3Daemon: After the OpenClaw Gateway daemon service is installed, it will run continuously in the background and can receive commands from platforms like Telegram, WhatsApp.
install.step4Desc: Now you can start using OpenClaw!
